report.executive_summary: Bottom line: NowTrendIn's instrument partially reflects human attention today, but it FAILS the no-gap test on the data's most important live data point and on a systematic class of already-arrived topics. On 2026-06-22 Alan Greenspan died at 100 — the day's premier, globally wall-to-wall mainstream event — and the engine scored it Detection 91.8 / Confidence 75.6 / gap +16.2, the single largest positive gap in the 100-topic sample, landing a confirmed obituary in the "very early, not-yet-confirmed" Anomalies band. That is precisely the broken behavior the product's own no-gap principle says invalidates the gap metric. The failure is systemic, not a fluke: every mainstream nation and mega-cap outside a privileged top tier collapses to a near-identical ~45 Detection / ~67 Confidence signature badged EMERGING (china, india, russia, france, apple, openai, korea), with a uniform ~-22 LAGGING gap — Confidence systematically exceeding Detection on topics that have unambiguously already arrived, the inverse of the intended "early = Detection leads" design. The single biggest risk is therefore credibility at the already-arrived end of the curve: the dual-pathway anti-collapse gate demonstrably works at the very top (trump 94.6/94.2, FIFA 93.7/93.7, iran 86.1/90.1, election 94.7/94.3) but does not extend across the full mainstream band. Separately, a real data-integrity bug exists: the stored heisenberg_gap field contradicts its own Detection−Confidence on the entire mcp family (det−conf ≈ +1.5 but stored gap −23), directly breaking the "both scores explain each other" test. The good news that must be weighed against this: the scores DO discriminate (monotonic stage ladder, 520/595 distinct values, gap converging to ~0 only for genuinely-arrived BREAKOUT topics), the engine does NOT fabricate confidence for niche topics (a one-directional failure mode), and the no-N-into-Gradient firewall — the core integrity rule — is correctly enforced in live code. The headline recommendation: do NOT make any public "before it arrives" lead claim until (a) a non-circular Wikipedia+GDELT no-gap anchor is wired as a held-out validator and the mainstream-collapse + gap-incoherence bugs are fixed, and (b) the accuracy ledger clears its product-success bar (the moat charter's own bar is currently CONTRADICTED at 0.0% honest hit rate). The instrument is promising and structurally honest in its design, but it is not yet a defensible, ledger-backed "human attention" instrument across the full diffusion curve. Caveat on scope: this audit rests on a 100-topic no-gap sample within a 2,103-topic snapshot and an 88-instrument market pull, all single-day (2026-06-22/23) — directionally decisive but not a multi-week out-of-sample validation.
report.what_it_is: The Gradient Score is an "attention intelligence" instrument that emits TWO headline numbers from ONE measurement set, built on an explicit (not metaphorical) Heisenberg trade-off: a trend's POSITION on the diffusion curve (how early/niche it is) and its MOMENTUM (whether it will sustain) cannot both be measured at maximum precision at once, because waiting to confirm momentum is the act of measurement that destroys earliness. DETECTION is the earliness-optimized score (accepts ~22% false positives, audience: creators/marketers); CONFIDENCE is the precision-optimized score (target <9% FP, audience: institutional analysts/investors). The GAP = Detection − Confidence is treated as the single most precise read of where a topic sits on the curve: a large positive gap = detected early, not yet broadly confirmed; the gap closes UPWARD as a genuine trend matures (both converge HIGH = "confirmed, act now") and closes DOWNWARD as a false positive collapses (both fall LOW). Displayed STAGES are Detection-derived with exact cutoffs: BREAKOUT ≥85, STRONG 70–84, EMERGING 55–69 (shown to users as "INDICATING" — display-only rename, internal key stays EMERGING), MARGINAL/WATCHING 35–54, MONITORING <35; ANOMALY is an orthogonal flag, not a strength band, firing when Detection runs 16+ points ahead of Confidence. A THIRD score, N (nowtrendin_score), measures internal in-app query demand and is the ranking key for the "Now TrendIn" view (while "All Signals" ranks by Detection); critically, N is EXCLUDED from the Gradient Score itself to prevent a reflexive search→score→search feedback loop. DOC-vs-CODE reconciliation, with drift flagged: (1) the docs/CLAUDE.md and even in-code comments still cite headline weights of G 0.40 / D 0.25 / I 0.20 / M 0.10 / C 0.05 for Detection and an N weight of 10–12% — these are STALE; live code runs a six-component (G/I/M/D/C/P, plus Persistence) N-excluded set renormalized to 1.0, with N at 0.0 everywhere. (2) METHODOLOGY.md names the displayed bands "BREAKOUT/STRONG/EMERGING/WATCHING/MONITORING" while signals.ts uses MARGINAL as the primary 35–54 label and renames EMERGING→INDICATING — a minor doc lag. (3) "Overall" is defined two different ways: weighted-six at scoring time vs (Detection+Confidence)/2 at serve/calibration time, so /scores and /detail can diverge. (4) The legacy five-component gradient_engine_backend.py (no P, no N) is NOT the serve path and must not be cited as authoritative; the canonical live scorer is gravitational_anomaly_detector.py.
report.factors_and_weighting: The score is built from six external measurement components, each scored 0–100. INTENT (May-2026 Heisenberg PDF, faithfully built in the legacy 5-component module): G — Gradient Strength (niche-to-mainstream engagement density ratio; fires instantly from ~8 signals); I — Inertia (consecutive 6h windows of accelerating signal count; highest-latency, best sustain predictor); M — Medium/Platform diversity (cross-platform diffusion pattern match); D — Dark Matter (probabilistic hidden-private-conversation inference from first-timer ratio, engagement asymmetry, vocabulary clustering); C — Confidence Decay (freshness/age, Heisenberg-exempt). The live canonical engine adds P — Persistence (cycles held above EMERGING). LIVE CODE WEIGHTS (renormalized six-component, N=0): Detection = G 0.375 + D 0.216 + I 0.182 + M 0.102 + C 0.057 + P 0.068; Confidence = I 0.278 + P 0.267 + M 0.222 + G 0.122 + C 0.067 + D 0.044; Overall = G 0.244 + I 0.222 + M 0.167 + D 0.133 + C 0.078 + P 0.156. The structure is faithful to intent (Detection leads on the earliest-firing G+D; Confidence leads on the slowest-to-confirm I+P+M), but WEIGHT DRIFT is real and material: published/comment weights (G 0.40, N 10–12%) do not match live constants, and the in-code comment block at apply_calibration lines 1100–1101 lists weights that match NEITHER the docs NOR the code below it. DUAL-PATHWAY: every topic is scored on the expert G·I·M·D·C·P composite by default, then a dual-pathway blend runs last with weight w = mainstream_ratio (baseline-relative deviation from the topic's OWN 12-cycle footprint). Mainstream-tier evidence lands in G's DENOMINATOR (suppresses the early read) and raises w; expert/niche tier keeps w≈0 and preserves the expert gradient (the moat). This is the correct design — fame ≠ movement — and it visibly protects the top tier (FIFA would otherwise collapse to 27.6). WHERE WEIGHTING IS SUSPECT: (1) the same-class ESTABLISHED discount has THREE different multipliers depending on maturity source (0.25 / 0.40 / 0.45) — a calibration inconsistency. (2) Overall and Stage are computed on different inputs at scoring vs serve time, so a topic can carry two different stages. (3) The mainstream-collapse evidence (see error analysis) shows the dual-pathway/calibration preservation is NOT firing for mid-mainstream rows. CIRCULARITY ASSESSMENT: the headline integrity rule is upheld — N (in-app demand) is verifiably excluded from all three composites, so there is NO forbidden internal-feedback leak; ranking-by-N is a display choice that does not contaminate the score. Dark Matter uses external-platform (Reddit) first-timer/asymmetry signals, NOT NowTrendIn's own users — acceptable. The ONE real concern: PERSISTENCE is self-referential — it is derived from topic_lifecycle counters built from the engine's own prior OVERALL scores crossing the EMERGING threshold, and P then feeds Overall (0.156) and Confidence (0.267). Inputs G/I/M/D/C are independent, so it is feedback rather than pure circularity, but P is NOT an input independent of what it validates — the classic violation of the project's own "validator must use independent inputs" standard, and a standing item to watch.
report.error_analysis: This is where the instrument is weakest. THE DECISIVE FAILURE (basis: WebSearch-confirmed against the Federal Reserve official statement + CBS/NBC/ABC): Alan Greenspan died 2026-06-22 at 100 — the day's #1 confirmed mainstream story — and scored Detection 91.8 / Confidence 75.6 / gap +16.2, the sole large positive gap in the sample and squarely in the Anomalies band. The no-gap principle explicitly defines this exact case (an obviously-mainstream, fully-confirmed topic read as early/uncertain) as the broken state that invalidates the gap metric. The likely mechanism: Detection is correctly high, but Persistence has not accrued for a one-day-old event, depressing Confidence — the documented "gap closes as P rises" lifecycle — but for a wall-to-wall confirmed obituary the instrument must not read it as a speculative lead. This needs a same-surge/breaking-news check. THE SYSTEMATIC FAILURE (basis: internal distribution + footprint counts): seven mainstream nations/mega-caps collapse to a near-identical signature — china 45.6/67.5 (219 mentions/11 platforms), india 45.6/67.5, russia 45.5/67.3, france 45.3/67.5, apple 45.2/67.2, openai 45.4/67.1, korea 45.4/67.1 — all badged EMERGING with a uniform ~-22 LAGGING gap; obama is worse (24.3/56.7, WATCHING, gap -32.4). Confidence systematically exceeds Detection on already-arrived topics, the inverse of the intended design, indicating Detection is being clamped below what the footprint justifies and that the dual-pathway preservation gate is not firing for mid-mainstream rows. CONFIRMED ERROR (adversarially verified, severity medium): "strait of hormuz" reads Detection 23.9 / WATCHING while the shorter "hormuz" token reads STRONG 72.3/83.2 — the SAME story (the dominant 2026 oil/geopolitics flashpoint, IEA-described as the largest supply disruption in oil-market history). Root cause verified in code: normalize_topic() only lowercases/strips punctuation and there is no alias-consolidation layer for non-AI topics, so surface-form variants are scored as independent topics; the canonical full phrase accumulates fewer hits and collapses. CONFIRMED ERROR (verified, severity low): "belgium vs iran" (a real 6/21 World Cup fixture) reads 10.2/17.7 MONITORING — under-weight but explainable by a known, DOCUMENTED, fix-pending mechanism (CLAUDE.md sec 15 M/D reweighting names this exact case as IN DESIGN). Note: the auditor's proposed "wide positive gap" expectation here is wrong — a fully-arrived mainstream event should read HIGH on both axes, not with a wide gap; the auditor conflated mainstream with early-emerging. DATA-INTEGRITY BUG (basis: arithmetic): the stored heisenberg_gap contradicts Detection−Confidence on the entire mcp family (mcp 74.5−73.0 = +1.5 but stored gap −23; same on claude code, claude-code, model-context-protocol, mcp server) — a ~24pt internal contradiction breaking the "both scores explain each other" test, independent of the mainstream question. ACCURATE EXAMPLES (the engine genuinely works here): trump 94.6/94.2/+0.3, FIFA 93.7/93.7/0.0, iran 86.1/90.1/-4, election 94.7/94.3/+0.4, canada 90/91.7/-1.7, claude 77.8/86.2 STRONG — all high+high+aligned, the correct already-arrived read. NEGATIVE CONTROLS also pass: SpaceX IPO 2026 (24.9/25.4, n=0) and obscure dev-tool slugs (fastapi 46.2/48.6, langchain 45.5/47.9) sit LOW on BOTH axes — the engine does NOT fabricate confidence for niche topics, so the failure is ONE-DIRECTIONAL (it strips score from mid-mainstream topics; it does not invent it for niche ones). SATURATION/SEPARATION (basis: 2,103-topic distribution): the instrument is NOT collapsed — stage medians ladder monotonically (Detection 90.5/74.1/46.0/32.8/17.3), 520/595 distinct Detection/Confidence values exist, and the gap converges to ~0 only for genuinely-arrived BREAKOUT topics (median -0.8). Real caveats: heavy near-duplicate string variants score identically (inflating apparent compression and the catch-all), some single-platform GitHub slugs carry mid-range Detection on one source, and a few thin BREAKOUT/anomaly rows ("bonkers", 5 mentions/4 platforms, 86–91 Detection) warrant a corroboration check. HONEST SAMPLE/AGE CAVEAT: all of the above is a single-day snapshot (2026-06-22) over a 100-topic hand-checked sample within 2,103 topics; it is directionally decisive (it fails on the day's #1 event plus a reproducible 7-topic pattern) but is not a multi-week out-of-sample false-positive measurement, and the stated 22%/<9% FP rates remain design targets, not ledger-confirmed.
report.market_signal_analysis: The Market Signal tier ladder (ELEVATED ≥80 / ACTIVE 60–80 / BUILDING 40–60 / ROUTINE 25–40 / DORMANT <25, on the Detection+Confidence average, with CALIBRATING overriding) is conceptually sound and the engine's baseline-relative design is genuinely strong: every component is scored as a z-score against the item's OWN history (z=0→0.30, z=2→~0.74), which correctly fixes the size-bias bug where mega-caps auto-scored ELEVATED on coverage volume alone. The CALIBRATING short-circuit is well-implemented and hard to bypass (a fresh IPO like SPCX trips all components and is honestly flagged "snapshot, not a confirmed signal"), which is exactly the cold-start honesty an institutional buyer should want. BUT as surfaced today the tier system does NOT track or separate instruments coherently. From the live 88-instrument pull (all fresh, scored within 0.7h — staleness is NOT the problem): the headline tier (market_gradient.tier / risk_stage) is ROUTINE for 87/88 (98.9%) — only alphabet is BUILDING — so the user-facing tier carries essentially ZERO information. THREE tier fields disagree on the same row: tier vs risk_stage vs classification (e.g. cre = ROUTINE tier but ELEVATED classification; recession, spacex, jpmorgan = ROUTINE tier but WATCH classification). There is no single source of truth. COLD-START CONTRADICTION: 28.4% of instruments are flagged calibrating=true, yet 0% have sufficient_baseline=false — every calibrating row simultaneously reports "enough baseline." The two gates are computed on different inputs (calibrating ≈ thin total_signals 1–6; sufficient_baseline ≈ cycle count) and are never reconciled. VALUE QUANTIZATION: Detection snaps to a few discrete buckets (20 instruments at 34.6, 16 at 32.2, 9 at 33.9), and calibrating rows collapse to floor pairs (29.9/29.9, sig=1) presented in the same shape as measured reads. ABNORMALITY IS NOISE-DOMINATED on thin coverage: the nine "highest abnormality" instruments (abn 4.8, delta 5.3%) are all 2-signal micro-cap floats (Lakewood Amedex, Boxlight, Utime) classed ROUTINE, while jpmorgan (abn 2.4, sig 125) is WATCH — so the "biggest movers" sort surfaces illiquid noise above real macro movers (recession, spacex). STRUCTURAL COVERAGE GAP: the tradable universe is a hardcoded ~16-name watchlist (15 mega-caps + SpaceX); anything off it silently zeroes positioning/fundamentals/momentum — the "SpaceX problem." PARITY RISK: two divergent market formulas coexist (the z-score engine that is actually persisted vs an absolute-magnitude inline variant tied to the Grade tool), so the Market tab and Grade tool can disagree for the same ticker — a CLAUDE.md 3-platform-parity violation. Also flagged: a hardcoded identical interpretation string across all instruments and double-encoded UTF-8 mojibake in the payload (will render as garbage on the terminal). NET: the engine is directionally honest about a quiet tape and its baseline-relativity is real, but the tiers as surfaced are uninformative and internally contradictory; "mostly ROUTINE" is defensible for a quiet market but is not the same as a tier system that is tracked accurately and surfaced coherently.
report.benchmarks_and_calibration: The no-gap test currently has no objective ground truth — it was scored by hand against WebSearch. The fix is a free, non-circular external anchor, and the clear winner is the WIKIPEDIA / WIKIMEDIA PAGEVIEWS API (AQS REST v1). It is the textbook attention ground-truth in published research, it is genuinely FREE, it is licensed CC0 1.0 (public domain — the cleanest possible position for a commercial product to ingest and even resurface), and it is FULLY non-circular: pageviews come from Wikimedia's own server logs, an independent population on a platform NowTrendIn does not collect from, sharing ZERO inputs with the Gradient Score (no RSS, no X scan, no blog feeds, no Detection/Confidence components). For the no-gap test it maps almost directly: an already-mainstream topic reliably has high, STABLE article traffic; a niche/pre-arrival topic has thin or no traffic. Pair it with GDELT DOC 2.0 (TimelineVol, free, percent-of-global-coverage) as an independent MEDIA-SUPPLY second axis — true mainstream should be HIGH on BOTH the demand axis (Wikipedia) and the supply axis (GDELT); disagreement is itself diagnostic (high GDELT + low Wikipedia = media-pushed, no public pull). Two critical exclusions on integrity grounds: X/Twitter is DISQUALIFIED as an anchor because it is already a scoring INPUT (the 6h scan) — using it as ground truth is the exact circular-metric failure the integrity standard forbids; and Google Trends must stay in its EXISTING lane as the breakout/arrival-timing anchor in the accuracy ledger — reusing it for no-gap would double-anchor to the same provider's attention definition and over-fit. Reddit (paid ~$12k/mo, opaque, and the wrong/early construct) and Google Ngram (annual, years-lagged, retrospective-only) are not live anchors. CONCRETE NON-CIRCULAR CALIBRATION LOOP (weekly, mirroring the accuracy-ledger cadence): STEP 0 — hard firewall: Wikipedia/GDELT data is written ONLY to a dedicated held-out table (attention_anchor) that NO Gradient-Score component, calibration weight, dual-pathway gate, or serve_payload ever reads. STEP 1 — resolve each scored topic to a canonical Wikipedia article (title-normalize + search; unresolved topics QUARANTINE to a review queue, never guessed — same condition-precedent discipline as the ingestion gate; this also directly attacks the surface-form fragmentation bug). STEP 2 — pull trailing ~90d daily pageviews + GDELT coverage-share; compute mainstream-LEVEL = log10(median daily views), STABILITY = low coefficient of variation, and coverage-share level. STEP 3 — label ALREADY-MAINSTREAM = pageviews above a high corpus percentile (~p80) AND stable AND high GDELT, EARLY = low+rising. STEP 4 — score the no-gap test as a confusion matrix; the key failure metric is FALSE-EARLY (we called something emerging that the anchors say is already mainstream — precisely the Greenspan/country-collapse failure). STEP 5 — calibrate DIRECTION only: a rising false-early rate prompts a HUMAN-reviewed adjustment of the SATURATION/ESTABLISHED-discount thresholds; the external number is never regressed into a weight. STEP 6 — a WORSENING drift alarm (like catchall_floor_log) trips when false-early climbs past a set delta. This stays permanently non-circular because the anchors live in a held-out table the score cannot read, share no inputs with the score, are never auto-fit, and are kept on a different external series from the breakout (Trends) job.
report.recommendations: QUICK WINS (ship-able now, no research gate, each integrity-preserving): (1) FIX the heisenberg_gap data-integrity bug — the stored gap must equal Detection−Confidence on every row; the mcp-family ~24pt contradiction is a flat arithmetic/storage bug and the easiest credibility win. Pin down whether the signed-vs-abs() divergence between scoring-time (signed) and apply_calibration (abs) is the culprit and standardize on signed. (2) ADD a surface-form alias/consolidation layer to normalize_topic()/extract_topics() so "hormuz / strait of hormuz / strait hormuz" and "mcp / model-context-protocol / mcp server" collapse to one canonical topic before scoring — fixes both the strait-of-Hormuz error and the duplicate-driven apparent saturation. (3) RECONCILE the three market tier fields to ONE surfaced source of truth, and reconcile the calibrating vs sufficient_baseline contradiction (gate both on the same definition). (4) GATE market abnormality on a minimum signal count so 2-signal micro-floats stop topping the mover ranking. (5) FIX the UTF-8 double-encoding mojibake in /risk/scores and replace the hardcoded identical interpretation string with a per-instrument read or remove it. (6) RECONCILE the stale weight comments (apply_calibration lines 1100–1101) and CLAUDE.md/docstrings to the actual live six-component weights, and align METHODOLOGY.md band names (MARGINAL/INDICATING) — documentation honesty. (7) RESOLVE the two divergent market formulas (z-engine vs absolute-magnitude inline) to a single persisted engine so the Market tab and Grade tool agree (parity rule). RESEARCH-GATED (require backtest-before-ship, must not bypass integrity): (8) BUILD the breaking-news / same-surge override for the no-gap top end so a high-Detection, confirmed, wall-to-wall event (Greenspan) is not read as a speculative lead — this likely means letting Detection-and-corroboration, not slow-accruing Persistence, drive Confidence for confirmed mega-events; validate it does not re-open false positives. (9) EXTEND the dual-pathway anti-collapse preservation across the full mainstream band so china/india/apple/openai stop collapsing to the ~45/67 EMERGING signature — diagnose whether their baseline-relative w is falling too low or the serve-time preservation gate is mis-firing; gate behind the new Wikipedia/GDELT no-gap precision report. (10) WIRE the Wikipedia+GDELT held-out no-gap calibration loop (Section above) BEFORE any public no-gap claim. (11) ADDRESS the Persistence self-reference: either down-weight P or add an independent persistence proxy, since it currently feeds on the engine's own past output (integrity-standard concern). (12) EXPAND the market universe beyond the 16-name watchlist via the market-coverage skill so trending real tickers aren't silently zeroed. (13) DO NOT publish any "before it arrives" lead figure until the accuracy ledger clears its own product-success bar (median lead ≥+3d, LED ≥35%, FP ≤30%, ≥100 matured events) — it is currently CONTRADICTED at 0.0% honest hit rate, and the moat charter itself requires printing FAIL until the denominator supports the claim.
report.verdict: Not yet — NowTrendIn cannot today make an unqualified "measures where human attention is moving before it arrives" claim to an institutional buyer, because the instrument fails its own no-gap credibility floor on the day's single most important confirmed event (Greenspan, +16.2 anomaly gap) and on a systematic class of already-arrived mainstream topics that collapse to a ~45/67 EMERGING signature, while the moat's lead claim is currently UNPROVEN and at last ledger CONTRADICTED (0.0% honest hit rate). What the product CAN defend right now is real and not trivial: a structurally honest dual-score design faithfully implemented in code, a verified no-N-into-Gradient firewall (the core integrity rule), genuinely baseline-relative market scoring with disciplined cold-start honesty, clean monotonic separation across 2,103 topics, demonstrably correct already-arrived reads for the famous top tier (trump/FIFA/iran/election), and a one-directional failure mode that never fabricates confidence for niche topics. For this to become a defensible "human attention" instrument, the following must be true: the heisenberg_gap and surface-form-fragmentation bugs are fixed; the dual-pathway anti-collapse protection extends across the full mainstream band; a held-out, non-circular Wikipedia+GDELT no-gap anchor is wired and shows acceptable false-early precision; the market tier fields and cold-start flags are reconciled into a single coherent surface; and the accuracy ledger clears its own published success bar so the "before it arrives" lead is denominator-backed rather than a claim. Until then it is a promising, honestly-architected pre-validation instrument — sellable as a measurement tool with explicit caveats, but not yet as a proven attention-prediction moat.

philosophy.heisenberg_principle: Applied exactly (not metaphorically): a trend has two simultaneously-desirable properties that cannot both be measured with maximum precision at once — POSITION on the diffusion curve (where the topic is, from private Discord/expert Reddit to mainstream) and MOMENTUM (whether it will sustain). The earlier you detect (high position certainty), the less time-window data exists to confirm momentum; the longer you wait to confirm momentum (high certainty), the later your detection. Waiting for confirmation IS the act of measurement that disturbs earliness. This is structural, not an engineering limit — ∆x·∆p ≥ ħ/2 has a 'reduced Planck constant' analog: an irreducible minimum uncertainty no better instrument removes. The founders' core claim: any tool promising BOTH maximum earliness AND maximum certainty is either measuring something other than it claims, or lying. The engineering response is to emit TWO scores from ONE measurement set — Detection (optimized for earliness, ~22% FP) and Confidence (optimized for precision, <9% FP) — and to treat the GAP between them as the single most precise read of where the topic sits on the curve (large gap = very early; closing gap = confirming; small gap = high conviction either way). Genuine trends converge HIGH over ~24h; false positives converge LOW.
philosophy.dark_matter_framing: Dark Matter (D) is the framing that the attention exists but our direct instruments cannot yet observe it — most of the conversation that precedes a mainstream surge happens in PRIVATE/unseen channels (DMs, Discords, group chats) that no public scraper sees. The task is therefore not to wait for visible volume but to find the right METRICS that infer the unseen mass from its observable effects: first-timer ratio (new participants suddenly flowing into a niche = external traffic from invisible referral), engagement asymmetry (comments/upvote anomalies), and vocabulary clustering/compression artifacts. The PDF makes the epistemic point explicit: D 'fires early but reads probabilistically — not because of insufficient data, but because it measures the shadow of something invisible. More data cannot resolve this uncertainty.' This is why D is weighted HIGH in Detection (25%) and LOW in Confidence (5%). The Gravitational Anomaly Detector is the dedicated instrument for this: 'a gravitational anomaly, by definition, is something detected from its effects rather than its direct observation — that is a Heisenberg measurement. You know something is there. You cannot yet confirm its shape.' The v2.0 roadmap extension of this (CLAUDE.md sec 15) routes research/early-signal outlets to the D pathway via platform_tier, not is_organic.
philosophy.gimdc_weights: Five components, OVERALL weights: Gradient Strength (G) 30% · Inertia (I) 25% · Medium Sequence (M) 20% · Dark Matter (D) 15% · Confidence Decay (C) 10% (sums to 100). The same five measurements are re-weighted into the two Heisenberg modes by their trade-off profile. DETECTION (earliness-led, leads with the earliest-firing components): G 0.40 + D 0.25 + I 0.20 + M 0.10 + C 0.05. CONFIDENCE (precision-led, leads with the slowest-to-confirm components): I 0.35 + M 0.30 + G 0.20 + C 0.10 + D 0.05. Rationale per component: G fires immediately from 8 signals (instant position certainty → top Detection weight); I needs 18–24h / 4 windows of acceleration (highest-latency, best sustain predictor → top Confidence weight); M fires partial-match early but full Pattern A/B/C match needs time (Detection accepts partial 0.10, Confidence requires full 0.30); D fires early but probabilistic (high Detection 0.25, low Confidence 0.05); C is the only component exempt from the Heisenberg trade-off (it measures the measurement's freshness/age, a fact not a prediction → identical 0.05/0.10-ish behavior in both modes).
philosophy.before_it_arrives_premise: The product's entire premise and tagline: 'the first instrument that measures where human attention is moving before it arrives.' Competitors (Google Trends, Brandwatch/Meltwater, Exploding Topics) all sit at the SAME point — maximum certainty, minimum earliness — because they fire only when volume crosses statistical significance vs baseline, by which point the trend has been building for days and is downstream of awareness (search happens AFTER people already heard). NowTrendIn's differentiation is detecting the gradient at the niche/expert/private stage BEFORE mainstream volume exists, accepting quantified false-positive cost (Detection ~22%) as the price of earliness. The moat charter (nowtrendin_agent_SKILL_moat) states this bluntly: 'before it arrives' is a CLAIM until something measures it — the Nightly Audit Agent exists to turn the claim into a measured, auditable lead-vs-Google-Trends fact with receipts, and is required to print 0.0% / FAIL when the lead isn't there. The premise is only legitimate when denominator-backed; no public lead/accuracy claim may exceed what a current accuracy-ledger epoch supports.
philosophy.no_gap_principle: A true measurement instrument must NOT only catch early/niche signals — it must ALSO correctly peg topics that have ALREADY arrived in the mainstream, reading them with high Detection AND high Confidence (small gap, both converged HIGH = 'confirmed, act now' per the 0–15pt 'Both Agree — High Conviction' band). The convergence lifecycle encodes this: a genuine trend's gap closes UPWARD as both scores rise to agreement; a false positive's gap closes DOWNWARD as both collapse. An instrument that could only fire on the speculative dark-matter end (60+pt gap) but mis-read an obviously-mainstream, fully-confirmed topic as low/uncertain would be broken — it would fail the 'both scores explain each other' test that makes the score 'an instrument rather than an opinion.' The no-gap principle is thus the credibility floor: correct behavior is required at BOTH ends of the diffusion curve, and the Detection/Confidence/gap triple must remain coherent for already-arrived topics, not just emerging ones. This is mirrored in the engine's dual-pathway gate (which keeps mainstream topics from collapsing the score) and in the calibration auditor's directional-leak test (a score ABOVE what G/I/M/D/C justify is a leak; a score BELOW is the engine's own legitimate clamp).
intended_metrics: G — Gradient Strength: niche-to-mainstream engagement density ratio (0–100), where signal is concentrated; fires instantly from ~8 signals
I — Inertia Confirmation: consecutive 6-hour windows of accelerating signal count + vocabulary expansion (sustained-momentum score 0–100); Detection fires at 2 windows, Confidence requires 4

M — Medium Sequence: cross-platform diffusion-pattern match (Builder→Buyer / Enthusiast→Mainstream / Research→Commerce = Pattern A/B/C, 0–100); Detection accepts partial match, Confidence requires full

D — Dark Matter Inference: probabilistic hidden-private-conversation score (0–100) from first-timer ratio (≥25% Detection / ≥40% Confidence), engagement asymmetry, vocabulary clustering / compression artifacts
C — Confidence Decay: signal freshness + score-trajectory direction (0–100); the measurement-quality / age axis, Heisenberg-exempt
Detection Score (earliness-optimized, ~22% FP) and Confidence Score (precision-optimized, <9% FP) — the two headline outputs
Heisenberg Gap = |Detection − Confidence| with banded interpretation (0–15 high-conviction / 16–35 confirming / 36–60 very-early / 60+ speculative dark-matter-only)
Diffusion pattern label (A/B/C) and lead-time-estimate (days before mainstream / Google Trends)
First-timer ratio, engagement-asymmetry flag, false-positive risk (low/med/high), confidence_level — supporting dark-matter and reliability fields
Accuracy-Ledger lead vs Google Trends with literal receipts (the moat metric); Gravitational Anomaly Detector firing at widest-gap dark-matter signals
gaps_between_intent_and_build: INTENT FAITHFULLY BUILT (the core): transfer/gradient_engine_backend.py compute_gradient_score (lines 602–665) implements the canonical model verbatim — overall = G*.30+I*.25+M*.20+D*.15+C*.10; detection = G*.40+D*.25+I*.20+M*.10+C*.05; confidence = I*.35+M*.30+G*.20+C*.10+D*.05. The dual-weight vectors are also frozen in calibration methodology (transfer/gradient_engine_extensions.py lines 48–50). This matches the May-2026 Heisenberg Analysis PDF exactly.

GAP — Dark Matter is partially instrumented vs the vision. compute_dark_matter_score (backend lines 550–571) derives first_timer_ratio from REDDIT signals only (reddit_sigs), and D = first_timer*0.45 + asymmetry*0.35 + vocab*~0.20. The 'private conversation / invisible mass' framing and 'compression artifacts' named in the PDF are not yet fully realized as independent metrics — D currently leans on Reddit first-timer + a binary asymmetry flag. The richer dark-matter inference (cross-source first-timer, referral/external-traffic inference) is still roadmap (CLAUDE.md sec 15 platform_tier D-routing is IN DESIGN, not shipped).

GAP/EVOLUTION — A THIRD headline that the original dual-score doc did not contemplate: N (nowtrendin_score, internal query-demand). The build adds N as the ranking key for the 'Now TrendIn' view (CLAUDE.md sec 13), separate from Detection-ranked 'All Signals'. The intent doc framed the product as a DUAL score (Detection+Confidence+gap); N is a later addition. It is correctly quarantined (signal_calibration_integration.py line 1227–1230 stores it as nowtrend_internal; now_trending_direction.py line 21 explicitly states N 'is NOT used here … independent of the score'), and the diagnostics actively guard against N leaking into the G/I/M/D/C headline (trend-signal-diagnostic 'N-discipline reconcile' + 'what-if-N inversion'). So it is a deliberate, firewalled extension rather than a violation — but it is a divergence from the pure two-score vision and a standing leak risk the agents must police.

GAP — The 'before it arrives' premise is, per the moat charter, UNPROVEN and at last ledger CONTRADICTED (0.0% honest hit rate, resolved calls lagging Google Trends). The instrument to PROVE the lead exists (Nightly Audit Agent / accuracy ledger) is built and is correctly designed to print FAIL, but the product-success bar (median lead ≥+3d, LED ≥35%, FP ≤30%, ≥100 matured events) is not yet cleared. The build faithfully measures the claim; the claim is not yet validated.

GAP — Confidence-level / FP-risk thresholds in the build (backend lines 626–631) are heuristic (confidence≥70 & I≥50 ⇒ high) rather than the empirically-calibrated <9% / ~22% FP targets the PDF asserts. The stated false-positive rates are design targets, not yet ledger-confirmed out-of-sample.

ALIGNED — The Heisenberg GAP as primary information IS built: signal_calibration_integration.py computes gap = |detection−confidence| and build_gap_interpretation gives a DIRECTIONAL (not size-only) label/meaning (lines 1215–1216, 1323–1324), matching the PDF's gap-band semantics and the convergence-lifecycle intent.

model_doc.detection_definition: DETECTION = "earliness." Per METHODOLOGY.md: "Weighted toward the components that fire first (Gradient Strength, Dark Matter). Optimized for speed; accepts more false alarms. Audience: creators, marketers, trend-forward brands." It is one of "the two public scores (the Duality)," produced from the same six external components (G/I/M/D/C/P) as Confidence but with different emphasis. Stored as velocity_scores.detection_score. The DISPLAYED stage band is derived from Detection in the app (stageFromScore in frontend/lib/signals.ts) — the same basis the category tiles use, so a topic's badge can't contradict its category tile. "Now TrendIn" view ranks by N; "All Signals" ranks by Detection.
model_doc.confidence_definition: CONFIDENCE = "precision." Per METHODOLOGY.md: "Weighted toward components that require sustained, repeated evidence (Inertia, Persistence, Platform Diversity). Audience: institutional analysts, investors, strategic planners." Same six external components as Detection, weighted differently. The doc explicitly states Confidence "weights P [Persistence] heavily — it is one of the strongest precision drivers, and the gap closes as P rises." Stored as velocity_scores.confidence_score.
model_doc.overall_formula: OVERALL = "a balanced blend, used for stage classification on the engine side" (METHODOLOGY.md). All three public scores (Detection, Confidence, Overall) are composed by the canonical scorer gravitational_anomaly_detector.py from "the six external components G/I/M/D/C/P only, renormalized to sum to 1.0" — N is EXCLUDED. EXACT WEIGHTS ARE NOT PUBLISHED: the doc's governance/trade-secret rule states "exact component weights, thresholds, and calibration constants live in code only (server-side, env-overridable). They are never published here, in the UI, or in any API response." So the docs give the STRUCTURE (six components, sum-to-1.0 renormalization, Detection leans G+D, Confidence leans I+P+M) but not the numeric blend coefficients. Engine stores an overall-based signal_stage (surfaced as engineStage); the app's displayed stage uses Detection instead.
model_doc.gap_definition: GAP = Detection − Confidence (a SIGNED lead, not an absolute gap). METHODOLOGY.md: "The gap (Detection − Confidence) is the earliness signal: a large gap = detected early, not yet broadly confirmed; it closes as Persistence rises." Stored as velocity_scores.heisenberg_gap. The Anomalies category in signals.ts fires on (detection − confidence) >= 16 — i.e., Detection running 16+ points AHEAD of Confidence. Note explicitly: "Lagging topics where Confidence exceeds Detection are NOT anomalies" (the lead is signed/positive-only).
model_doc.stage_thresholds: Displayed stage band is Detection-based (stageFromScore in frontend/lib/signals.ts), with these exact numeric cutoffs and display ranges (STAGE_LEGEND): BREAKOUT = score >= 85 (range "85–100", color #00C896 green, "Strongest signal"); STRONG = 70–84 (>=70 && <85, #2D7EEF blue, "High signal strength"); EMERGING = 55–69 (>=55 && <70, #D4A017 gold, "Building signal") — DISPLAYED to users as "INDICATING" (internal key/type/colors stay EMERGING; display-only rename via stageLabel which maps EMERGING→INDICATING); MARGINAL = 35–54 (>=35 && <55, #E85A1E, "Early / unconfirmed"), with WATCHING kept as a legacy back-compat alias of MARGINAL; MONITORING = below 35 (the else/default, #9AA3B0 muted, "Low-intensity background signal"). ANOMALY is a separate ORTHOGONAL axis (not a strength band): "Detection ahead of Confidence by 16+ pts," filter (detection − confidence) >= 16, color #8B5CF6 purple — "an anomaly can sit at any stage; what defines it is the early-edge lead, not the level."

factors.name: G — Gradient Strength (a.k.a. Niche Concentration)
factors.feeds: Detection (named as a first-firing component, 'Weighted toward Gradient Strength, Dark Matter'); also a Confidence input. M-vs-D router: mainstream tier lands in G's DENOMINATOR (suppresses early read); expert/niche tier = G numerator.
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED — exact weight lives in code only (trade-secret rule). Relative emphasis: heavy in Detection. Stored as velocity_scores.gradient_strength + gradient_ratio.

factors.name: I — Inertia / Momentum
factors.feeds: Confidence (named among 'Inertia, Persistence, Platform Diversity' that require sustained evidence).
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED (code only). Discrete fallback I = max_consecutive·25 only affects <2-cycle topics; established (>=2 cycle) topics use continuous momentum_engine I. Stored as velocity_scores.inertia_score.

factors.name: M — Platform Diversity
factors.feeds: Confidence (sustained-evidence cluster). Also fed by mainstream-tier breadth/magnitude in the dual-pathway.
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED. Documented calibration note: previously pinned all 4+-platform topics at a flat M=90 (caused Detection freezing); fixed 2026-06-11 with a continuous curve anchored at 4→90 climbing toward 100 (env PLATFORM_DIVERSITY_CONTINUOUS). Stored as velocity_scores.platform_diversity.

factors.name: D — Dark Matter
factors.feeds: Detection (named first-firing: 'Gradient Strength, Dark Matter'). D is the expert/niche-tier pathway (G numerator, w~0).
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED. Documented mechanics: concave first-timer curve 100·ratio^0.7; quality gate = 0.4 + 0.6·organic_ratio (is_organic scales D's quality gate only); is_first_timer is D's numerator. Stored as velocity_scores.dark_matter_score (+ first_timer_ratio, engagement_asymmetry).

factors.name: C — Confidence Decay
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED (code only). Stored as velocity_scores.confidence_decay.

factors.name: P — Persistence
factors.feeds: Confidence HEAVILY ('one of the strongest precision drivers'); Detection 'barely uses P (speed matters, not history)'. Gap closes as P rises.
factors.weight: NOT PUBLISHED numerically, but documented behavior: brand-new topic P=0 (no history, no penalty); a topic above EMERGING for ~7+ consecutive cycles (~3 days of hourly scoring) approaches P=100. Stored as velocity_scores.persistence_score.

factors.name: N — Now Trending (nowtrendin_score)
factors.feeds: NEITHER public Detection nor Confidence — EXCLUDED from the trend Gradient Score on the canonical serve path. Only feeds the separate 'Now Trending Gradient Score' what-if and the Grade tool.
factors.weight: Excluded from the six-component sum-to-1.0 blend. In the what-if only: score_with_n = score·(1−w_eff) + N·w_eff, where w_eff is scaled down by external-evidence sufficiency. Stored as velocity_scores.nowtrendin_score.

model_doc.dual_pathway: DUAL-PATHWAY (dual_pathway.py; pipeline stage COLLECT→…→SCORE→CALIBRATE→DUAL-PATHWAY). Two routes calibrated separately so fame ≠ movement (scores are baseline-relative — deviation from a topic's own norm). The DECISIVE ROUTER is platform_tier, NOT is_organic: (1) tier='mainstream' feeds M (dual_pathway breadth/magnitude), lands in G's DENOMINATOR (suppresses the early read), and raises blend weight w → this is the mainstream/'arrived' pathway. (2) tier in {'expert','niche'} → the Dark-Matter / expert-gradient pathway: zero mainstream breadth, w~0, G numerator → the early/pre-viral pathway. is_organic only scales D's quality gate (quality = 0.4 + 0.6·organic_ratio); is_first_timer is D's numerator. News-outlet corroboration sets mainstream_confirmed; tier-migration is tracked. Hard rule: backtest_dual_pathway.py must pass ALL cases before any calibration ship (backtest-before-ship). IN DESIGN (not shipped): reputable source = ½ weight, FULL only on ≥2 DISTINCT reputable source_names; research/early-signal outlets (War on Rocks, Rest of World, Global Issues, Pew, RAND, NBER) routed to Dark Matter via GHOST_FEEDS at expert/niche tier rather than _news_write (which forces mainstream and would suppress them). Stored columns: detection_pathway, mainstream_ratio, mainstream_breadth, mainstream_confirmed, n_mainstream_platforms, tier_migration. The FIFA calibration analysis documents that scoring is tuned for expert→mainstream TECH diffusion; consumer-origin already-mainstream events score near the bottom.
model_doc.n_score_role: N (nowtrendin_score) = "Now Trending (internal demand)" — how often Now TrendIn users query a topic (volume + recency spike). It is EXPLICITLY "NOT a component of the public Gradient Score." Three documented placements: (1) Trend Gradient Score (the product sold to institutions, canonical gravitational_anomaly_detector.py → velocity_scores → /scores): N EXCLUDED — deliberate, to avoid a reflexive feedback loop (users search → score rises → they search more) and preserve external-world objectivity. (2) 'Now Trending Gradient Score' what-if (trend signal page only): shows what the score would be if N were folded in as score_with_n = score·(1−w_eff) + N·w_eff; w_eff is scaled down by external-evidence sufficiency to prevent the thin-data trap, with a demand_driven transparency flag ('limited external confirmation'); clearly labeled separate/demand-inclusive and NEVER sold as the Gradient Score. (3) Grade tool (ai_grade.py): N INCLUDED intentionally (G/I/M/D/C/P + N) as a demand-aware estimate for data-thin topics — a different, labeled metric. Two open items flagged: verify graded topics actually have LOW N (likely the opposite), and the Grade tool should carry the same thin-data N down-weighting + flag. Also: in the app, the 'Now TrendIn' view RANKS by N (proprietary nowtrendin_score), while 'All Signals' ranks by Detection.
model_doc.detail: SOURCES & GOVERNANCE: METHODOLOGY.md is declared the single source of truth for which components each scorer uses; it explicitly withholds numeric weights/thresholds as trade secrets ('structure and relative emphasis, not the numbers'). So no concrete blend coefficient is documented — the only hard NUMBERS the docs give are: stage cutoffs (85/70/55/35) and ranges (85–100, 70–84, 55–69, 35–54, <35); anomaly gap threshold 16; first-timer curve 100·ratio^0.7; D quality gate 0.4 + 0.6·organic_ratio; what-if formula score·(1−w_eff)+N·w_eff; discrete I fallback max_consecutive·25; the (fixed) M-saturation flat 90 at 4 platforms; FIFA-analysis 88–100 AI-taxonomy floor (ai_topic_intelligence.py). THREE SCORER MODULES (only one canonical): gravitational_anomaly_detector.py = G/I/M/D/C/P, N excluded = CANONICAL live serve path (writes velocity_scores); ai_grade.py = G/I/M/D/C/P + N = Grade tool only; gradient_engine_backend.py = G/I/M/D/C (no P, no N) = LEGACY 5-component, NOT on serve path, 'do not cite as authoritative.' DISPLAY vs ENGINE: app displays Detection-based stage (stageFromScore); engine stores an overall_score-based signal_stage (engineStage) for reference. EMERGING is displayed as INDICATING (display-only rename; internal key stays EMERGING). WATCHING = legacy alias of MARGINAL. model_code.detection_formula:
CODE (gravitational_anomaly_detector.py score_topic, lines 3918-3926), 6 EXTERNAL components renormalized to 1.0, N EXCLUDED:
detection = min(100, G*0.375 + D*0.216 + I*0.182 + M*0.102 + C*0.057 + P*0.068)
where G=gradient_strength, D=dark_matter, I=inertia, M=platform_diversity, C=confidence_decay, P=persistence. Then (for mainstream/blended topics only) overwritten by the dual-pathway blend in score_all_topics (line 4327): detection = (1-w)*expert_detection + w*md, where md=mainstream_detection(mag,M,I,P,breadth)=soft_cap(mag*0.55 + breadth*28.0 + M*0.10 + I*0.05) (dual_pathway.py:192-196), w=mainstream_ratio.
model_code.confidence_formula:
CODE (lines 3929-3936), N EXCLUDED, P weighted near-highest:
confidence = min(100, I*0.278 + P*0.267 + M*0.222 + G*0.122 + C*0.067 + D*0.044). For mainstream/blended topics overwritten (line 4333) by blend: confidence = (1-w)*expert_confidence + w*mc, where mc = soft_cap(mag*0.32 + abs_breadth*28.0 + P*0.25 + M*0.15) (dual_pathway.py:285).
model_code.overall_formula: CODE (lines 3908-3916): overall = min(100, G*0.244 + I*0.222 + M*0.167 + D*0.133 + C*0.078 + P*0.156). For mainstream/blended overwritten (line 4328): overall=(1-w)*expert_overall + w*mo, mo=soft_cap(mag*0.45 + abs_breadth*28.0 + P*0.18 + M*0.15 + I*0.08) (dual_pathway.py:277). NOTE the SERVE-TIME recompute in signal_calibration_integration.apply_calibration uses DIFFERENT overall logic: new_overall=(new_detection+new_confidence)/2 (line 1140), NOT the weighted-6 formula — a structural divergence between scoring-time and serve/calibration-time overall.
model_code.gap_formula: CODE: gap = round(detection - confidence, 1) at scoring time (line 3954, stored as heisenberg_gap). After dual-pathway blend recomputed: heisenberg_gap = round(blend.detection - confidence_score, 1) (line 4334-4335). In apply_calibration the gap used for the gap-interpretation label is abs(new_detection - new_confidence) (line 1215) — note ABS there vs signed at scoring time. Doc CLAUDE.md frames gap as 'D - C' conviction; code stores signed detection-minus-confidence.
model_code.stage_logic: CODE thresholds (lines 485-488): BREAKOUT>=85, STRONG>=70, EMERGING>=55, WATCHING>=35, else MONITORING. Applied THREE places on different inputs: (1) score_topic on `overall` (lines 3987-3996); (2) dual-pathway post-blend on the blended `_ov`/overall (lines 4346-4351); (3) apply_calibration RE-DERIVES stage from avg=(new_detection+new_confidence)/2, NOT overall (lines 1158-1168) — so the calibration-layer stage can disagree with the scoring-layer/dual-pathway stage because one keys off overall and the other off the det/conf average. DISPLAY note: per CLAUDE.md the 'EMERGING' stage label is renamed to 'Indicating' at the frontend, and 'Now TrendIn' view ranks by N (nowtrendin_score) while 'All Signals' ranks by Detection — but stage itself is computed from Detection-derived scores, not N.

factors.name: G — Gradient Strength (niche/expert engagement density vs mainstream density)
factors.weight: Detection 0.375, Confidence 0.122, Overall 0.244 (doc CLAUDE.md/code comments say '30% / 0.40' — DRIFT)
factors.how_computed: compute_gradient_strength (3309-3358): niche_ratio = niche_eng/(niche_eng+mainstream_eng); score=min(100, niche_ratio*100*(0.65+0.35*count_bonus)), count_bonus=min(1,len(signals)/15). No mainstream signal => raw_ratio=10 ('perfect gradient'). Then apply_signal_count_modifier multiplies G (can exceed 100, re-capped 0-100 at line 3890). For ESTABLISHED topics G is discounted by calibration multiplier (x0.25-0.45).

factors.name: I — Inertia (acceleration across 6h windows)
factors.how_computed: compute_inertia (3360-3407): bins signals into 6h windows, counts consecutive growing windows (15% tolerance, counts[i]>=counts[i-1]*0.85), growth_score=min(100, max_consecutive*25). OVERRIDDEN by momentum_engine.compute_inertia when >=2 cycles of stored history exist (lines 3867-3871).
factors.data_source: signal extracted_at timestamps; momentum_engine reads stored velocity_scores history

factors.name: M — Platform Diversity (distinct platforms)
factors.how_computed: compute_platform_diversity (3409-3451): continuous curve for 4+ platforms base=100-40/n_plat (env PLATFORM_DIVERSITY_CONTINUOUS=1), legacy map {1:20,2:50,3:80,4+:90} otherwise; +15 if spans niche AND mainstream; +10 for GitHub+HN+Reddit (Pattern A).

factors.name: D — Dark Matter (hidden activity inference)
factors.how_computed: compute_dark_matter (3453-3519): evidence = ft_score*0.65 + asym_score*0.35, ft_score=100*(ft_ratio^0.7), asym_score=70 if asymmetry else 0; quality gate=0.4+0.6*organic_ratio; dark_score=evidence*quality. ft_ratio needs >=3-signal sample.
factors.data_source: is_first_timer, comments/upvotes (asymmetry), is_organic flags on signals

factors.name: C — Confidence Decay
factors.how_computed: compute_confidence_decay (3521+). Lowest-weighted component across all three composites.

factors.name: P — Persistence (cycles held above EMERGING)
factors.how_computed: compute_persistence (3565-3635): rate_score=(cycles_above_emerging/total_cycles)*60 + streak_score=min(40, current_streak*6.5). Reads topic_lifecycle counters that are themselves derived from prior OVERALL scores (>= EMERGING_THRESHOLD).
factors.data_source: topic_lifecycle table (total_scoring_cycles, cycles_above_emerging, current_streak) — built from prior overall_score history

factors.name: N — NowTrendIn Score (internal user query demand)
factors.weight: 0.0 in all composites — DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (lines 3900-3906). Code comment cites N×0.10/0.12/0.10 as the OLD 7-component weights now removed.
factors.how_computed: compute_nowtrendin_score (3637-3715): vol_score=min(70, log1p(total_queries_30d)/log1p(100)*70) + recency_score(0-30) from 24h-vs-7d-baseline query spike. Computed and DISPLAYED separately, never folded into Detection/Confidence/Overall.
factors.data_source: topic_queries table (in-app user search counts) — INTERNAL/on-platform demand

model_code.dual_pathway_gate: The expert G·I·M·D·C·P composite is the DEFAULT. The dual-pathway blend (dual_pathway.blend) runs LAST in score_all_topics (lines 4296-4351) for EVERY topic, but its WEIGHT w=mainstream_ratio=max(breadth_factor, magnitude_factor) gates how much it applies. w is BASELINE-RELATIVE (deviation from the topic's OWN rolling median footprint over last 12 cycles), not absolute fame: breadth_factor=(cur_n - breadth_baseline)/_BREADTH_DELTA_FULL, magnitude_factor=(mag - magnitude_baseline)/_MAG_DELTA_FULL (35.0). Cold start (<MIN_BASELINE_CYCLES or no baseline): falls back to ABSOLUTE breadth/magnitude, flagged 'calibrating'. detection=(1-w)*expert_detection + w*md. pathway label: w>=0.6 mainstream, w<=0.2 expert, else blended. Expert-origin topics have mainstream breadth ~0 so w~0 and the expert gradient is preserved untouched (the moat). SEPARATELY, in apply_calibration the serve-time 6-component RECOMPUTE (lines 1122-1140) is GATED to expert/niche/'' pathway only (line 1151-1155): for stored mainstream/blended rows it PRESERVES the incoming dual-pathway detection/confidence/overall instead of recomputing — preventing the documented collapse of all mainstream topics to ~25-28 (FIFA 27.6, Trump 25.8).
doc_vs_code_drift: WEIGHT DRIFT: Component docstrings/CLAUDE.md state G=30%/0.40, I=25%, M=20%, D=15%, P=15% (e.g. compute_gradient_strength docstring says '30% weight'; compute_nowtrendin_score docstring says 'N — 10% of Overall, 12% of Detection, 10% of Confidence'). ACTUAL live code weights are the 6-component renormalized sets (Overall G0.244/I0.222/M0.167/D0.133/C0.078/P0.156; Detection G0.375/D0.216/I0.182/M0.102/C0.057/P0.068; Confidence I0.278/P0.267/M0.222/G0.122/C0.067/D0.044). N is 0 everywhere, contradicting the docstrings that still claim N is 10-12% of each.

apply_calibration comment block (lines 1100-1101) documents Detection as 'G(0.40)+D(0.25)+I(0.20)+M(0.10)+C(0.05)' and Confidence as 'I(0.35)+M(0.30)+G(0.20)+C(0.10)+D(0.05)' — these stale comment weights DO NOT match the actual code constants just below (G*0.375 etc) NOR include P. The comment is documentation drift inside the code itself.

OVERALL formula divergence: scoring-time overall = weighted-6 (line 3909); serve/calibration-time overall = (detection+confidence)/2 (line 1140). Same topic can carry two different overall definitions depending on path.
STAGE input divergence: scoring + dual-pathway derive stage from `overall`; apply_calibration derives stage from avg of detection+confidence. Can disagree.

calibration multiplier mismatch: seed_known_topics writes ESTABLISHED multiplier 0.40 (line 517) but get_topic_maturity_state knowledge_base path returns 0.25 (line 695) and lifecycle path returns 0.45 (line 756) for ESTABLISHED — three different discounts for the same class depending on source.
GAP sign: scoring stores signed detection-confidence; apply_calibration gap-label uses abs() — sign information lost for the directional label.
circularity_risks: NO N-INTO-GRADIENT LEAK (good): N (nowtrendin_score, in-app query demand = on-platform engagement) is computed but EXPLICITLY excluded from Detection/Confidence/Overall (lines 3900-3906, all weights renormalized to 6 external components). This is the correct enforcement of the project's no-circular-metrics rule. Verified: no composite term references N/nowtrendin_score.

PERSISTENCE SELF-REFERENCE (medium): P (compute_persistence) is derived from topic_lifecycle counters (cycles_above_emerging, current_streak) which are themselves computed from prior OVERALL scores crossing EMERGING_THRESHOLD (_update_topic_lifecycle lines 3741, 3767-3776). Since overall includes P at weight 0.156 (and confidence at 0.267), the score partially validates itself across cycles: a topic scored high once is more likely to be counted 'above emerging', raising P, raising next cycle's overall/confidence. The inputs (G,I,M,D,C) are independent, so it is not pure circularity, but P is NOT an input independent of what it validates — it is a feedback term on the engine's own past output. This is the classic 'validator must use inputs independent of what it validates' concern from the project's integrity standard.

DARK MATTER uses on-platform engagement asymmetry (comments/upvotes) and is_first_timer — these are external-platform (Reddit/etc) engagement signals, NOT NowTrendIn's own users, so NOT the forbidden internal-feedback circularity. Acceptable.

DISPLAY-RANKING N exposure (low): the 'Now TrendIn' view ranks topics by N per CLAUDE.md. N never enters the Gradient Score itself, so ranking-by-N does not contaminate the score — but it does mean the default-sorted list a user sees is ordered by internal demand, which could create a perception loop even though the underlying scores stay objective.

market_model.detection_formula:
Two coexisting Detection formulas (the engine and a deployed inline variant); the engine's is authoritative because score_all_risks calls market_signal_engine.apply_market_signal (financial_risk_gradient.py:2417).

CANONICAL ENGINE (market_signal_engine.py compute_market_signal, lines 145-180): Detection = round( Σ DETECTION_WEIGHTS[c] * scored[c].score for c in DETECTION_WEIGHTS ) * 100. DETECTION_WEIGHTS = {dark_positioning 0.30, positioning_concentration 0.25, analyst_signal 0.25, cross_market_diffusion 0.10, signal_freshness 0.10}. Each scored[c].score is the BASELINE-RELATIVE unit value (0-1) from score_component → _z_to_unit(z), NOT the raw component. So 'analysts + positioning' enter as z-scores vs the item's own history, then weight-sum × 100.

The three Detection inputs are assembled (assemble_market_components, lines 216-288):
- analyst_signal = norm( log1p(article_count)/log1p(50)*0.6 + |avg_sentiment|*0.3 + min(creator+broadcast cov,8)/8*0.1 ) [Alpha Vantage news vol+tone + creator/broadcast coverage]
- positioning_concentration = norm( min(|short_interest change_pct|,30)/30*0.4 + min(|13F shares_change_pct|,40)/40*0.4 + log1p(stage1_insider_count)/log1p(20)*0.2 ) [FINRA shorts + WhaleWisdom 13F + SEC/Finnhub Form-4]
- dark_positioning = norm( stress_v*0.6 + min(|repo_volume_change_pct|,20)/20*0.4 ) where stress_v = 0.85 high/severe, 0.6 moderate, 0.35 mild, 0.2 none [OFR funding stress + repo].

DEPLOYED INLINE VARIANT (financial_risk_gradient.compute_market_gradient, lines 2020-2120) computes Detection DIFFERENTLY — on ABSOLUTE 0-100 magnitudes, NOT z-scores: analyst = mclamp(log1p(art)*18 + |sent|*35 + creators_cov*6 + min(broadcast,5)*4); pp (Positioning Pressure) = mclamp(|si_chg|*4 + |inst_chg|*4); ba (Baseline Abnormality) = the positioning-engine score. detection = weighted avg of present parts (analyst 0.40, pp 0.35, ba 0.25, re-normalized over present weights). This is the function tied to MARKET_TIERS, but it is NOT the one persisted in the live loop.
market_model.confidence_formula:
CANONICAL ENGINE (compute_market_signal): Confidence = round( Σ CONFIDENCE_WEIGHTS[c] * scored[c].score ) * 100, with CONFIDENCE_WEIGHTS = {fundamental_confirmation 0.35, market_momentum 0.30, cross_market_diffusion 0.20, signal_freshness 0.15}. Again each input is the baseline-relative z-unit value.

Confidence inputs (assemble_market_components):
- fundamental_confirmation = norm(mean of present parts) over: FMP score_normalized (primary, real P&L), beneficiary cycle_inflection, Finnhub sustainability/100. If no parts → 0.0.
- market_momentum = norm(mean of present parts) over: valuation_rerating, min(|price_return_12m|,2.0)/2.0. If no parts → 0.0.
- cross_market_diffusion = norm(min(venue_count,6)/6) [shared with Detection].
- signal_freshness = norm(1 - newest_age_hours/168), 1-week linear decay; defaults to 0.4 if age unknown [shared].

DEPLOYED INLINE VARIANT (compute_market_gradient): fu = mclamp(cycle_inflection*100) or None; pr = mean of [valuation_rerating*100, |price_return_12m|*40]; ma = OFR stress mapped 85/60/35/30. confidence = weighted avg (fu 0.45, pr 0.40, ma 0.15) over present parts; if NONE present, falls back to the diffusion engine's confidence_decay/confidence_score ('diffusion confirmation, no ticker fundamentals'). NOTE the inline variant's fundamentals = cycle_inflection ONLY and ignores FMP/sustainability, unlike the engine.

In BOTH: gap = detection − confidence. Detection >> Confidence = early window; both high = confirmed; both low = routine.

tiers.name: ELEVATED
tiers.rule: avg = (detection+confidence)/2 >= 80. Both MARKET_LEVELS (engine, market_signal_engine.py:86) and MARKET_TIERS (inline, financial_risk_gradient.py:2009) use identical cutoffs.

tiers.name: DORMANT
tiers.rule: avg < 25. The lowest tier. Note: tier is purely a function of the AVERAGE of detection+confidence; the gap_state (EARLY/CONFIRMING/CONFIRMED/LAGGING/MIXED) is a SEPARATE classification layered on top, not the tier.

tiers.name: CALIBRATING (overrides all)
tiers.rule: any_calibrating = True (ANY of the 7 components has fewer than MIN_BASELINE_TRUSTWORTHY=10 cycles, or fewer than MIN_BASELINE_CYCLES=3 so no z at all). When set, gap_state is forced to CALIBRATING by _interpret_gap regardless of scores; the numeric tier (DORMANT..ELEVATED) is still emitted but the read is flagged 'baseline forming / snapshot, not a confirmed signal'. SPCX (fresh IPO) is the canonical case — thin 13F/short history.

tiers.name: WATCH / UNUSUAL (DIFFERENT engine — Positioning, not Market Signal)
tiers.rule: These belong to the Positioning engine (revised_risk_positioning_gradient.py / inline positioning at fr_gradient.py:1286), NOT the Market Signal dual score. Per-item positioning_score: ROUTINE<25, WATCH<50, ELEVATED<72, UNUSUAL>=72, plus CALIBRATING when baseline_cycles<3 (score held to 12.0). The Market section ranks by this positioning_score (get_risk_scores sort key, line 2521), so the displayed market tier and the positioning classification are two distinct labels on the same card.

market_model.baseline_relative:
'Baseline-relative' is the core integrity principle: every component is scored against the ITEM'S OWN history as a z-score, never as an absolute magnitude — so a mega-cap doesn't auto-score high just because it always has heavy coverage/filings (the size-bias bug the design review fixed, where Nvidia/Meta/Alphabet all read ~77 ELEVATED on nothing but size).

Mechanism (score_component, market_signal_engine.py:117-137): for a component with samples >= MIN_BASELINE_CYCLES(3): z = (current - mean) / stdev where stdev = max(0.05, baseline.stdev). Then score = _z_to_unit(z): z<=0 → max(0.05, 0.30 + z*0.08) (below baseline floors low); z>0 → min(1.0, 0.30 + z*0.22), so z=0→0.30, z=2→~0.74, z=3+→~0.96. The result carries baseline_relative=True only if NOT calibrating. If samples<3, it returns a non-baseline fallback score = norm(current)*0.6 with baseline_relative=False, calibrating=True.

Baselines are built per-item, per-component from market_signal_history (get_market_baselines, line 341): take all rows for the item, identify distinct cycles by (signal_date, signal_time), SKIP the most-recent cycle (compare 'now' vs 'normal-before-now'), take the next `lookback`=12 cycles, compute mean/stdev/samples per component. With <2 samples stdev defaults to max(0.08, mean*0.3); a 0.05 floor is applied. Seeds: backfill_from_finra (positioning_concentration from ~12 bi-monthly FINRA points) and backfill_from_finnhub (fundamental_confirmation from ~12 quarters) so a name isn't CALIBRATING for days on first sight.
market_model.calibrating_rule:
An instrument/component is flagged CALIBRATING in two stacked situations:
1. NO usable baseline — fewer than MIN_BASELINE_CYCLES (3) cycles of history for that component. score_component returns the fallback norm(current)*0.6, calibrating=True, baseline_relative=False. The z-score is not even computed.
2. THIN/UNTRUSTWORTHY baseline (the cold-start guard, Fix #1 from the market diagnostic) — the component has >=3 but < MIN_BASELINE_TRUSTWORTHY (10, env MARKET_MIN_BASELINE) cycles. A z IS computed and kept for transparency, but calibrating=True is set because with so few samples the 0.05 stdev floor dominates and FABRICATES the z (the SPCX case: 5 cycles, floor-bound on 6 of 7 components). baseline_relative is set to NOT calibrating, i.e. False here.

Roll-up (compute_market_signal:151): any_calibrating = any component calibrating. Because every component is recorded every cycle, sample counts are uniform across an item, so a fresh IPO trips ALL components at once (reads CALIBRATING) while an established name's deep baseline never false-triggers. When any_calibrating, _interpret_gap forces gap_state='CALIBRATING' with text 'Building this item's baseline … treat as a snapshot, not a confirmed market signal' — this short-circuits BEFORE any ROUTINE/EARLY/CONFIRMED branch, so a fresh IPO can never be reported as a confident ROUTINE the data can't support.

factors.name: analyst_signal (Detection, weight 0.25)
factors.how_computed: norm(log1p(article_count)/log1p(50)*0.6 + |avg_sentiment|*0.3 + min(creators_covered+broadcast_channels,8)/8*0.1). Sources: Alpha Vantage news volume+tone, creator coverage (Meet Kevin/Andrei Jikh), broadcast coverage. Then scored baseline-relative.

factors.name: positioning_concentration (Detection, weight 0.25)
factors.how_computed: norm(min(|FINRA short change_pct|,30)/30*0.4 + min(|13F shares_change_pct|,40)/40*0.4 + log1p(stage1 insider count)/log1p(20)*0.2). Sources: FINRA short interest, WhaleWisdom 13F, SEC Form-4 / Finnhub insider (stage-1). The 'smart-money' factor.

factors.name: dark_positioning (Detection, weight 0.30 — highest)
factors.how_computed: norm(stress_v*0.6 + min(|repo_volume_change_pct|,20)/20*0.4); stress_v from OFR funding-stress label (0.85 high/severe → 0.2 none). Macro/cross-market shifts via OFR STFM repo + stress.

factors.name: fundamental_confirmation (Confidence, weight 0.35 — highest)
factors.how_computed: norm(mean of present: FMP score_normalized [primary, real P&L], beneficiary cycle_inflection, Finnhub sustainability/100). 0.0 if none present. (Inline variant uses cycle_inflection ONLY — divergence.)

factors.name: market_momentum (Confidence, weight 0.30)
factors.how_computed: norm(mean of present: valuation_rerating, min(|price_return_12m|,2.0)/2.0) from beneficiary live_inputs. 0.0 if none. Price/valuation trend.

factors.name: cross_market_diffusion (Shared)
factors.how_computed: norm(min(distinct venue_count,6)/6). Number of distinct sources/venues active on the item. Weight 0.10 detection / 0.20 confidence.

factors.name: signal_freshness (Shared)
factors.how_computed: norm(1 - newest_age_hours/168) [1-week linear decay]; defaults 0.4 when age unknown. Weight 0.10 detection / 0.15 confidence.

weaknesses: COLD-START / thin-baseline is acknowledged but the floor itself fabricates z: the 0.05 stdev floor (max(0.05, raw_stdev)) means a component whose true variance is near-zero still produces a large z on any small absolute move. The MIN_BASELINE_TRUSTWORTHY=10 guard only FLAGS this (calibrating), it does not correct the score — so once an item crosses 10 cycles the floor-fabricated z silently becomes a 'trusted' baseline_relative read with no smoothness check.

STALE / NARROW UNIVERSE: the tradable universe is the hardcoded WATCHLIST_TICKERS (15 mega-caps + SpaceX). Any company not on the watchlist gets no FINRA/13F/FMP/sustainability/beneficiary enrichment in score_all_risks (the ticker = WATCHLIST_TICKERS.get(display) gate at 2315), so positioning_concentration/fundamentals/momentum silently collapse to 0 for everything else — a structural coverage gap (the 'SpaceX problem' the market-coverage skill exists to catch). New IPOs are only covered if manually added.

TWO DIVERGENT MARKET FORMULAS coexist: market_signal_engine.compute_market_signal (z-score based, the one actually persisted via apply_market_signal at 2417) and financial_risk_gradient.compute_market_gradient (absolute-magnitude, MARKET_TIERS, used by market_signal_for_company / Grade tool at 2237). They use different inputs (e.g. fundamentals = FMP+bene+sustainability vs cycle_inflection-only) and different math, so the Grade tool's market read can disagree with the Market tab read for the same ticker — contradicting the CLAUDE.md 3-platform parity requirement.

BASELINE WINDOW IS SHALLOW AND MIXED-CADENCE: lookback=12 cycles but FINRA seeds are bi-monthly, Finnhub quarterly, live cycles ~6-hourly. Mean/stdev are computed over whatever rows exist regardless of cadence, so a baseline can blend 12 quarters of fundamentals with 12 6-hour cycles of positioning — the 'cycle' is not time-normalized, distorting z for slow-moving components.

ZERO-AS-DATA ambiguity: missing fundamental_confirmation / market_momentum default to 0.0, which the baseline machinery then treats as a real low observation. A name with no FMP data looks identically 'fundamentally weak/quiet' to one with genuinely poor fundamentals — Confidence is depressed by absence of data, not by data showing absence of strength.
SIGNAL_FRESHNESS default 0.4 when age unknown injects a mid-value into BOTH scores for items where date parsing failed — a silent fabricated input rather than abstaining.

GAP THRESHOLDS DIVERGE between the two engines (engine: EARLY gap>=36 & det>=45; inline interpretation: gap>=18 early) so the same detection/confidence pair yields different narrative states depending on which path renders it.

POSITIONING vs MARKET SIGNAL conflation on the card: get_risk_scores ranks by positioning_score (a different engine/threshold set: WATCH/UNUSUAL) while the headline tier comes from the Market Signal average (DORMANT..ELEVATED). A user sees two scoring vocabularies on one instrument, which can contradict (e.g. positioning UNUSUAL but market tier ROUTINE).

trend_stats.saturation: PARTIAL saturation, but the scores DO separate. Across all 2103 topics: Detection median 23.5 (p25=16.1, p75=34.6, std 15.7, range 3.8-100); Confidence median 40.4 (p25=23.8, p75=52.3, std 18.0, range 7.0-100). 69% of topics have Detection in 15-45 and 35% sit in the 25-45 band, so the bulk IS compressed in the low-to-mid range — but this is expected for an "early-signal" instrument where most topics are genuinely not moving. The discriminating evidence: 520 distinct Detection values and 595 distinct Confidence values across the set, and the stage medians ladder cleanly and monotonically (Detection 90.5 BREAKOUT > 74.1 STRONG > 46.0 EMERGING > 32.8 WATCHING > 17.3 MONITORING). The top of the scale is NOT pinned: BREAKOUT topics reach 94.7 Det / 94.3 Conf, far above the median mass. Conclusion: not collapsed/saturated — the band is dense at the bottom because most topics are quiet, but the instrument cleanly elevates the ~12% that are actually surging (BREAKOUT+STRONG+EMERGING = 157 topics). One caveat: there is visible value-clustering — top Detection integer buckets are 24 (97 topics), 20 (93), 22 (90), 38 (76) — and many string-variant duplicates of the same concept (e.g. claude code / claude-code / mcp / mcp server / model-context-protocol) land on identical scores (74.5 Det / 67.8 Conf recurs), which inflates apparent compression.
trend_stats.gap: heisenberg_gap is overwhelmingly NEGATIVE: 1983 of 2103 topics (94%) have gap<0, only 112 positive and 8 exactly zero. Distribution: median -11.4, p10=-27.7, p25=-22.2, p75=-6.3, p90=-2.9, min=-34.6, max=+17.6, std 10.0. Large gaps are common: 876 topics (42%) have |gap|>15 and 334 have |gap|>25. Gap behavior is stage-coherent: it is near-zero at BREAKOUT (median -0.8, i.e. Detection and Confidence have converged) and widens to its most negative at WATCHING (-22.4) and EMERGING (-21.6), where Confidence sits well above Detection — consistent with the documented 'gap reflects topics living in expert/niche communities or awaiting mainstream confirmation' semantics. The rare positive gaps (Detection > Confidence) flag thin/anomalous reads: e.g. 'bonkers' (gap +9.2, only 5 mentions, 4 platforms) and 'Alan Greenspan' (gap +16.2). Net: gap is a real second dimension, not noise, and it collapses toward zero exactly as topics mature to BREAKOUT.
trend_stats.separation: The scores DO discriminate. Three independent signals confirm separation: (1) monotonic stage ladder on Detection (90.5/74.1/46.0/32.8/17.3) and Confidence (91.0/80.3/66.8/51.7/25.3) with no inversions; (2) wide spread — Detection spans 3.8-100 with std 15.7, Confidence 7.0-100 with std 18.0, and 520/595 distinct values respectively; (3) gap converges to ~0 only for the genuinely-arrived BREAKOUT cohort. Recognizable mainstream topics rank where you'd expect for late-June 2026 (Iran/Hormuz/Israel conflict, 2026 FIFA World Cup/Qatar, Trump, Keir Starmer/Andy Burnham UK politics, Claude/Anthropic/AGI/ASI). WEAKNESSES that downstream agents should note: (a) heavy near-duplicate string variants of the same concept score identically (claude code vs claude-code; mcp/mcp server/model-context-protocol all 74.5/67.8; ai agent/ai agents/ai-agent/agent ai), inflating the catch-all and the apparent compression; (b) several single-platform GitHub-topic-slug rows (developer-tools, ai-tools, vector-database, platforms=1) carry mid-range Detection on essentially one source; (c) some thin BREAKOUT/anomaly rows ('bonkers' 5 mentions/4 platforms) score 86-91 Detection, so very-low-mention high-score rows warrant a corroboration check. 291 gravitational anomalies (14% of set) and 857 is_anomaly=True rows exist as a separate flag layer.
